headline_prefix: Your Back Pain Keeps Coming Back.
headline_highlight: A Nobel Prize Winner Finally Found Why.
subheadline: The molecule your body stopped making after 50 — and why it explains every failed treatment.

body.alt: Woman sitting on the edge of her bed in the morning, struggling to stand due to back pain
body.caption: For millions over 50, the first attempt to stand has become the moment that defines the whole day.

body.text: If your back pain never fully goes away, painkillers stop working the moment you cut back, or you feel that shooting discomfort down your leg — this is for you.

body.html: Carol was 57 when her lower back pain and sciatic nerve discomfort forced her to stop going to church on Sunday mornings. Not because of anything dramatic — the wooden pews had become something she could no longer sit through. The pain had quietly taken over the decisions in her life. Where she sat. How long she stood. Whether she went at all.

She’d tried everything. Physical therapy. CBD. The big Costco tub of glucosamine. Ibuprofen every morning for two years until her doctor warned her about her kidneys. Every time she cut back, the back pain and leg discomfort came roaring back within 48 hours. None of it worked long-term. She had started to accept a smaller life. What she didn’t know — what most people don’t know — is that none of it was aimed at the actual cause.

body.text: Why Back Pain and Sciatica Keep Coming Back

body.alt: Bottles of pain medication and supplements on a table
body.caption: Everything in that drawer has a story. None of them ended the way you hoped.

body.html: Ibuprofen blocks the back pain signal while you take it. Stop taking it, the signal returns — because nothing was fixed. Stretching works on your muscles and posture, not on what’s driving the pain at the cellular level. CBD and turmeric target entirely different pathways. You didn’t fail these treatments. They were aimed at the wrong problem.

body.text: “The back pain keeps returning because the cellular switch that should turn it off has stopped working. That’s not aging. That’s a specific biological deficiency.”

body.html: Inside your nervous system, immune cells called glial cells activate whenever a nerve is compressed or a joint is inflamed. In a healthy body they switch back off. After 50, they get stuck in the “on” position — constantly flooding the lower back and sciatic nerve with inflammatory signals long after the original cause is gone. That is why the shooting pain down your leg comes back. That is why the morning stiffness never fully clears. Not because your spine is collapsing. Because a cellular alarm is stuck, and nothing you’ve tried has been designed to switch it off.

body.alt: Woman carefully holding a rail on stairs due to lower back and leg pain
body.caption: Back pain and sciatic discomfort make every step a calculation. Understanding the cellular cause changed everything.

body.html: Dr. Rita Levi-Montalcini won the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine in 1986. She spent her later career investigating how the body naturally resolves inflammation — not suppresses it, but actually finishes it and switches it off. Her research identified the molecule the body uses to do this: Palmitoylethanolamide (PEA). It is not a drug. It is a molecule your own body produces to tell overactive glial and mast cells to stand down.

In a landmark 2010 clinical trial of 636 patients with sciatic nerve discomfort, micronized PEA achieved 50% pain reduction within three weeks — with a Number Needed to Treat of 1.5. That means for every 2 people who took it, at least 1 experienced meaningful relief. Most prescription pain medications score between 3 and 6. PEA doesn’t block the back pain signal. It resolves the inflammation driving it. That is a different thing entirely.

body.text: “PEA is the body’s own natural autacoid. It instructs overactive immune cells around the lumbar and sciatic nerves to resolve their response and stand down — completing the inflammatory signal rather than simply muting it.”
body.cite: Based on the ALIAmide framework — Dr. Rita Levi-Montalcini, Nobel Laureate

body.html: One thing determines whether it works: micronization. Standard PEA is destroyed in the gut before reaching the bloodstream. Micronized PEA — ground to particles under 10 micrometers — survives digestion, enters circulation, and reaches the inflamed nerve tissue. Every trial showing results used the micronized form. If the label does not say micronized, the research does not apply.

items.text: The background noise turns down. Morning back stiffness is still there — but quieter. Getting off a chair without bracing. The shooting sensation down the leg is less sharp.
More consistent through the day. Many people reach for ibuprofen less — not because they decided to, but because they didn’t need it.
The biggest gains. Most people at this point have stopped keeping score. The back pain that used to run the day is no longer in charge.

body.html: At week five, Carol went back to her church. She sat through the whole service — no shifting, no planning an exit. “I didn’t realize how much I’d quietly given up,” she said, “until I started getting it back.”

reviews.text: Lower back pain and that shooting feeling down my left leg for three years. I was on Aleve every morning. Day eight, I walked to the mailbox and back without stopping. Five weeks in, I hadn’t touched the Aleve once. I didn’t plan to stop — I just stopped needing it.

Chronic lower back pain every day for three years. Day six I sat through my granddaughter’s school play — two hours — without shifting once. My husband said ‘you didn’t fidget.’ I cried in the car on the way home.

body.alt: Woman rising from a chair with ease, free from back pain
body.caption: Getting up without grabbing the armrest. For most people it starts in the first week.

Anyone who feels that shooting or burning sensation down the leg and has been told “it’s just age”

comments.name: Barbara Sinclair
comments.text: 67 years old with lower back pain and that awful shooting down my left leg for two years. Three doctors told me it’s just age. Three weeks in and my mornings are different. The first ten steps don’t feel like broken glass anymore.

comments.name: Helen Grainger
comments.text: Chronic back pain for three years. Aleve, CBD, turmeric, the big Costco glucosamine tub — none of it lasted. The explanation of why those things aim at the wrong target finally made sense of years of frustration. Week four now. Walked to the mailbox without stopping for the first time in I don’t know how long.
